headline: Pendidikan Islam Curriculum Review Stalls After 8-Year Delay
context: The Islamic education curriculum for national schools has not been updated since 2017 despite a scheduled 5-year review cycle.

cards.big: Islamic education curriculum unchanged since 2017 missing 2 scheduled review cycles
cards.sub: Content does not address contemporary ethical challenges facing Muslim youth

cards.big: JAKIM and MOE share jurisdiction over Islamic education content creating decision gridlock
cards.sub: Neither agency has sole authority to approve curriculum changes

cards.big: 78% of Form 5 Muslim students in 2025 survey want more applied ethics less rote learning
cards.sub: Student feedback consistently requests relevance to modern challenges

cards.big: Turkey modernized its religious education curriculum in 2018 adding critical thinking modules
cards.sub: Reform maintained religious content while incorporating analytical skill development

cards.big: Refusing to update religious education does not protect tradition—it risks irrelevance
Should Islamic education curriculum include contemporary bioethics and digital ethics modules
